page: Microsoft Azure D96ds_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Ddsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 96 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 48 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes 5669 GB of local disk capacity and is Premium Storage capable. It does not include any GPUs.

The x86_64 architecture of the Intel Xeon processor includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations. In benchmark evaluations, the D96ds_v6 demonstrates top-tier performance, placing in the top 10% for the Passmark CPU score, Geekbench multi-core score, and static web serving throughput. Multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and software compilation with clang are also strong, placing in the top 25%. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Passmark memory scores falling into the average category.

The server's memory subsystem delivers strong read and write bandwidth, reaching 257.11 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. This high throughput, combined with the 5669 GB local disk, supports data-intensive operations. Benchmark results show strong database performance, with Passmark database operations and Redis SET operations both ranking in the top 25%. Additionally, the processor's AMX instructions enable strong performance in LLM inference for medium-sized 7B models. This hardware profile is highly suited for relational databases, high-throughput web serving, software compilation, and medium-scale AI inference workloads.
description: The Microsoft Azure D96ds_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Ddsv6 family, powered by a 96-vCPU Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz on Microsoft Hyper-V. It features 384.0 GB of RAM, 5669 GB of local storage, and no GPUs. Built on the x86_64 architecture, the processor supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX extensions. Benchmarks reveal top-tier multi-core CPU performance, Passmark scores, and static web serving throughput, alongside strong memory bandwidth and database operation speeds. Conversely, single-core CPU and memory benchmark scores remain in the average tier. This resource density and high-capacity local storage make the D96ds_v6 highly efficient for multi-threaded workloads, including relational databases, static web serving, software compilation, and medium-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96ds_v6 is a general-purpose VM featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 5669 GB local storage, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for databases and web serving.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96ds_v6 is a 96-vCPU general-purpose VM with 384 GB RAM and 5669 GB local storage, optimized for multi-core database workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity general-purpose instance featuring ninety-six dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, extensive local storage, and advanced matrix acceleration for demanding multi-threaded workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor
384.0 GB of system memory paired with 5669 GB of local storage
Intel AMX and AVX-512 extensions for accelerated vector and matrix operations
Top-tier multi-core CPU, Passmark, and static web serving benchmark performance
Well-suited for relational databases, web serving, and medium-scale LLM inference
